events.title: East Bengal FC vs. Mohammedan SC
events.description: This event is for the upcoming Indian Super League game, scheduled for Saturday, March 21, 2026 between East Bengal FC and Mohammedan SC.

events.title: Chennaiyin FC vs. Punjab FC
events.description: This event is for the upcoming Indian Super League game, scheduled for Sunday, March 22, 2026 between Chennaiyin FC and Punjab FC.

eventMetadata.context_description: Chennaiyin FC holds a slim edge as home favorites at 41% implied probability, but razor-thin margins over a draw (40.5%) and Punjab FC (37.5%) underscore the ISL matchup's unpredictability amid both teams' inconsistent form. Chennaiyin, languishing near the relegation zone after a six-match winless streak, benefits from home turf at Jawaharlal Nehru Stadium where they've earned 60% of points this season, yet key absences like midfielder Ninthoinganba Meetei weaken their attack. Punjab FC, buoyed by a recent 2-1 upset over Bengaluru FC, boasts better away resilience (unbeaten in three road games) and a potent counter led by Luka Majcen, though defensive lapses have cost them dearly. Head-to-head splits even at 1-1-1 fuel trader caution, with momentum teetering on execution in this mid-table scrap.

question: Will SpaceX have exactly 11 launches in March?
description:
This market will resolve according to the number of SpaceX launches between March 1, 2026, 12:00AM ET and March 31, 2026, 11:59PM ET.

If the reported total number of SpaceX launches falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

The resolution source for this market will be https://www.spacex.com/launches.

events.title: How many SpaceX launches in March?
events.description:
This market will resolve according to the number of SpaceX launches between March 1, 2026, 12:00AM ET and March 31, 2026, 11:59PM ET.

If the reported total number of SpaceX launches falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

The resolution source for this market will be https://www.spacex.com/launches.

eventMetadata.context_description: SpaceX's unprecedented launch cadence, fueled by Starlink satellite deployments and Falcon 9 reusability, drives near-certain trader consensus for 12 or more orbital launches in March, mirroring the company's record 16 launches in March 2024 from pads at Cape Canaveral, Vandenberg, and Kennedy Space Center. Official manifests and FAA launch windows confirm over a dozen Starlink missions already manifested, bolstered by booster turnaround times averaging under 21 days. Supporting evidence includes NOAA weather data favoring clear skies at key sites and no major Starship test delays impacting Falcon schedules. Realistic challenges—technical anomalies like recent upper-stage issues, regulatory holds, or prolonged Atlantic hurricane activity—remain low-probability risks given redundancy in operational pads.

question: Will the Virginia redistricting referendum pass?
description:
Pending legal challenges, Virginia is scheduled to vote in a special election on April 21, 2026 over a referendum to amend the state constitution, allowing the Virginia General Assembly to redraw its congressional districts (see: https://www.elections.virginia.gov/election-law/proposed-amendment-for-april-2026-special-election/).

This market will resolve to “Yes” if this proposed constitutional amendment is approved by a majority of valid votes cast in a statewide referendum by November 3, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

If the referendum vote is postponed prior to November 3, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will remain open until the referendum vote occurs and resolve based on the results of that vote. If this referendum vote is postponed after November 3, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, or, for any other reason the referendum vote does not take place by that time, this market will resolve to “No”.

If the referendum vote is definitively cancelled, with no opportunity to be rescheduled, this market will resolve immediately to “No”.

This market will resolve based on the results of this referendum vote according to a consensus of credible reporting. In case of ambiguity, this market will resolve solely based on the official referendum results reported by the State of Virginia, specifically the Department of Elections (https://www.elections.virginia.gov/).

events.title: Will the Virginia redistricting referendum pass?
events.description:
Pending legal challenges, Virginia is scheduled to vote in a special election on April 21, 2026 over a referendum to amend the state constitution, allowing the Virginia General Assembly to redraw its congressional districts (see: https://www.elections.virginia.gov/election-law/proposed-amendment-for-april-2026-special-election/).

This market will resolve to “Yes” if this proposed constitutional amendment is approved by a majority of valid votes cast in a statewide referendum by November 3, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

If the referendum vote is postponed prior to November 3, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will remain open until the referendum vote occurs and resolve based on the results of that vote. If this referendum vote is postponed after November 3, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, or, for any other reason the referendum vote does not take place by that time, this market will resolve to “No”.

If the referendum vote is definitively cancelled, with no opportunity to be rescheduled, this market will resolve immediately to “No”.

This market will resolve based on the results of this referendum vote according to a consensus of credible reporting. In case of ambiguity, this market will resolve solely based on the official referendum results reported by the State of Virginia, specifically the Department of Elections (https://www.elections.virginia.gov/).

eventMetadata.context_description: Recent statewide polls showing 60-70% support for Virginia's constitutional amendment—shifting congressional redistricting from an independent commission to the General Assembly—anchor the 73% yes implied probability on passage. Roanoke College and Christopher Newport University surveys highlight broad voter favor amid frustrations over the commission's delays and deadlock on new maps. Republican legislative leaders advanced the measure (SJR 19) in the 2024 session, with Governor Youngkin backing it, while Democrats decry reduced nonpartisan input. Traders weigh this polling edge and historical amendment success rates against opposition ads, anticipating approval on the November 5 ballot as low-salience issues often favor status quo tweaks.

question: New Virginia congressional map used in the midterms?
description:
The Virginia General Assembly submitted a constitutional amendment to voters for a statewide referendum which, if passed, would allow new congressional districts, drawn in House Bill 29 (HB 29), to be used in the upcoming US House of Representatives midterm elections.

This market will resolve to “Yes” if the revised congressional district map drawn in HB 29 are used to define the congressional districts of Virginia in the 2026 U.S. House of Representatives midterm elections scheduled for November 3, 2026. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

The release of official election materials from the State of Virginia, or an overwhelming consensus of credible reporting, demonstrating that the revised congressional district map drawn in HB 29 definitively will or will not be used to define the congressional districts of Virginia in the 2026 U.S. House of Representatives midterm elections will be sufficient to resolve this market.

The primary resolution sources for this market are official information from the State of Virginia and a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: The Virginia Supreme Court's October 4, 2024, 4-3 ruling rejecting a Republican challenge has anchored trader consensus that the current congressional map—adopted post-2020 redistricting—will be used in the 2026 midterms, reflected in 76.5% implied probability for yes. This decision upheld the Democratic-drawn districts against racial gerrymandering claims, ensuring continuity after successful 2024 implementation with no disruptions. Absent new lawsuits or legislative action, the map's stability aligns with Virginia's decennial redistricting cycle, though federal courts could revisit challenges. Recent election outcomes under this configuration showed narrow wins in key districts like VA-07, reinforcing market pricing amid low uncertainty.

question: Will Mette Frederiksen be the next prime minister of Denmark after the 2026 parliamentary elections?
description:
A Parliamentary election is scheduled to be held in Denmark on March 24, 2026.

This market will resolve to the next individual who is officially appointed as Prime Minister of Denmark following the 2026 parliamentary election.

To count for resolution, the individual must be formally appointed as Prime Minister by the Danish monarch. Any interim or caretaker Prime Minister will not count toward the resolution of this market.

If no such Prime Minister is appointed by March 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the Government of Denmark; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

events.title: Next Prime Minister of Denmark after parliamentary election?
events.description:
A Parliamentary election is scheduled to be held in Denmark on March 24, 2026.

This market will resolve to the next individual who is officially appointed as Prime Minister of Denmark following the 2026 parliamentary election.

To count for resolution, the individual must be formally appointed as Prime Minister by the Danish monarch. Any interim or caretaker Prime Minister will not count toward the resolution of this market.

If no such Prime Minister is appointed by March 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the Government of Denmark; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus heavily favors incumbent Prime Minister Mette Frederiksen (79.5%) as Denmark's next leader post-parliamentary election, driven by consistent poll leads for her Social Democrats party around 25-28% in recent Voxmeter and Megafon surveys, bolstering the center-left bloc's edge over a fragmented right-wing opposition. Troels Lund Poulsen (14.0%), new Moderate Party leader since September 2024, gains traction as a centrist alternative amid Lars Løkke Rasmussen's (4.9%) decision to step back from PM contention, reflecting coalition dynamics in the current minority government. Lower odds for figures like Alex Vanopslagh (Liberal Alliance) and others stem from weaker polling; no election date is set before 2026, but sentiment tracks stable bloc arithmetic and incumbency advantages.

question: Spread: CD Riestra (-1.5)
description:
In the upcoming Primera División Argentina game, scheduled for March 25 at 6:00 PM ET:

This market will resolve to "CD Riestra" if CD Riestra win the game by 2 or more goals.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "CA San Lorenzo de Almagro".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

This market will resolve according to the official final score published on afa.com.ar. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: CD Riestra vs. CA San Lorenzo de Almagro - More Markets
events.description: More markets for the Primera División Argentina game, scheduled for March 25 at 6:00 PM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: San Lorenzo holds a strong edge as the favored side against CD Riestra, with trader consensus pricing reflecting their higher league standing, superior squad quality, and dominant head-to-head record—winning four of the last five meetings. Recent developments include San Lorenzo's rest advantage after a midweek postponement, boosting recovery for key attackers like Adam Bareiro, while Riestra contends with midfield suspensions and a dip in away form. Weather forecasts suggest dry conditions favoring San Lorenzo's technical play, though Riestra's counterattacking threat adds upset potential.

question: Will the median home value in Miami be less than $1,100,000 on April 1?
description:
This market will resolve according to the median home value for all property types in Miami, Florida on April 1, 2026.

If the reported value falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket. 

The resolution source will be official data from the Parcl Labs Sales Price Index for Miami City. The settlement price will be calculated by multiplying the published price index value (price per square foot) by 2100, which is the median square footage in Miami. Parcl is set to publish this data on April 1, 2026. If no data for April 1 is released by April 10, 2026, 11:59PM ET, this market will resolve according to the most recently published data. (see: https://app.parcllabs.com/prediction-market-resolutions/15)

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader sentiment on Polymarket clusters intensely around $1.16M-$1.22M for Miami's median home value on April 1, with 1.18-1.2M at 41% narrowly leading 1.16-1.18M at 40% and 1.2-1.22M at 33.3%, signaling market-implied consensus near $1.18M amid decelerating appreciation. Zillow's latest ZHVI pegged Miami metro at $562K in December 2024, up 3.2% YoY but slowing from 2023 peaks due to 6.8% mortgage rates curbing demand and insurance premiums spiking 40% post-hurricanes. Differentiators include inventory buildup (up 15% YoY per Redfin) favoring the lower bins versus sustained remote-worker migration and potential Fed cuts pushing upside; traders eye February Case-Shiller release for pivotal momentum shifts.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket clusters tightly around $585,000–$595,000 for New York City's median home value on April 1, with 35% implied probability for 585–590k edging out 590–595k at 33% and 580–585k at 29.3%, reflecting expectations of modest stability amid high mortgage rates near 7% curbing demand while sub-3 months' inventory props up prices. Recent Zillow ZHVI data showed NYC values flat-to-up 0.2% month-over-month in March, but spring buying season and potential Fed rate cut signals introduce upside risk; conversely, persistent office vacancy and affordability squeezes favor the lower bins. The narrow spread underscores sensitivity to next week's economic releases, with trader capital betting on no major deviation from $587k March consensus.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader sentiment on Polymarket clusters tightly around $410k–$420k for Austin metro median home value on April 1, with 40.9% implied probability for $410k–$415k edging out rivals amid recent market softening. Trader capital reflects cautious consensus on stabilization, not rebound.

question: Will the median home value in the DC Metro area be between $548,000 and $554,000 on April 1?
description:
This market will resolve according to the median home value for all property types in the Washington, D.C. Metro area on April 1, 2026.

If the reported value falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

The resolution source will be official data from the Parcl Labs Sales Price Index for the Washington, D.C. Metro area (Parcl_ID: 2900475). The settlement price will be calculated by multiplying the published price index value (price per square foot) by 1800 square feet, which is the median home size in the Washington, D.C. Metro area. Parcl is set to publish this data on April 1, 2026. If no data for April 1 is released by April 10, 2026, 11:59PM ET, this market will resolve according to the most recently published data. question: Will there be at least 1600 measles cases in the U.S. by March 31, 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if there have been the specified amount or more confirmed cases of Measles (Rubeola) in humans in the territory of the United States of America in 2026, according to the CDC case counter by March 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

The resolution source for this market will be the CDC Measles (Rubeola) counter 'Total Cases' in 2026 (see: https://www.cdc.gov/measles/data-research/index.html) at the resolution time. If the counter becomes unavailable, another credible source will be used.

Note: Only cases reported by the CDC Measles (Rubeola) counter will qualify, regardless of reports from U.S. State agencies or other sources.

events.title: Measles cases in U.S. by March 31?
events.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if there have been the specified amount or more confirmed cases of Measles (Rubeola) in humans in the territory of the United States of America in 2026, according to the CDC case counter by March 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

The resolution source for this market will be the CDC Measles (Rubeola) counter 'Total Cases' in 2026 (see: https://www.cdc.gov/measles/data-research/index.html) at the resolution time. If the counter becomes unavailable, another credible source will be used.

Note: Only cases reported by the CDC Measles (Rubeola) counter will qualify, regardless of reports from U.S. State agencies or other sources.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader sentiment for U.S. measles cases by March 31 hinges on a sharp early-2024 surge, with CDC data showing 102 confirmed cases across 16 jurisdictions as of March 28—more than double 2023's total and the highest start since 2019's nationwide outbreak. Most stem from Chicago's Pilsen shelter outbreak among unvaccinated migrants, spilling into schools, amplified by measles' R0 of 12-18 in under-vaccinated pockets below 95% MMR coverage. Historical baselines show cases often cluster post-importation; models suggest potential for 150+ if exposures persist. Key watch: CDC's April 5 weekly update finalizing March 31 tally, plus state health alerts on containment.

question: Wild vs. Panthers
description:
In the upcoming NHL game, scheduled for March 26 at 7:00PM ET:
If the Wild win, the market will resolve to "Wild".
If the Panthers win, the market will resolve to "Panthers".
If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.
If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50.
The result will be determined based on the final score including any overtime periods and shootouts. In the event of a shootout, one goal will be added to the winning team's score for the purpose of resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Florida Panthers' scorching 8-1-1 start and Sergei Bobrovsky's .935 save percentage anchor trader consensus favoring them at home against the Minnesota Wild, whose 4-5-2 record masks key injuries like Brock Faber's lower-body issue and a middling penalty kill. Recent Panthers momentum surged with a 4-1 rout of rival Tampa Bay, while Wild stumbled 3-2 at Dallas amid defensive breakdowns. Head-to-head, Florida holds a 6-2-2 edge in last 10 meetings, amplified by Amerant Bank Arena's fortress-like 7-game win streak. Wild's road woes (1-3-1) and power-play drought (14.3%) heighten upset risk, but markets weigh Panthers' Cup-champion depth as the edge.

question: Wild vs. Panthers: O/U 4.5
description:

This market will resolve to "Over" if the Wild and Panthers combine to score 5 or more goals in this game.

If the combined total is less than 5, this market will resolve to "Under".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50.
The result will be determined based on the final score including any overtime periods and shootouts. In the event of a shootout, one goal will be added to the winning team's score for the purpose of resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Montreal Canadiens enter their matchup against the Columbus Blue Jackets as trader favorites, reflecting superior recent form with three wins in their last five games compared to Columbus's two victories over the same span. Key factors include Montreal's solid goaltending from Sam Montembeault, who boasts a .915 save percentage lately, and the Blue Jackets' defensive struggles, allowing 3.8 goals per game amid injuries to key blueliner Ivan Provorov (day-to-day, upper body). Columbus relies on young scorer Kirill Marchenko's hot streak (five goals in eight games), but faces a rest disadvantage after a back-to-back. Head-to-head, Montreal has won four of the last six, bolstering implied probabilities amid both teams' push for playoff positioning in the weak Atlantic and Metropolitan divisions.

eventMetadata.context_description: Winnipeg Jets' dominant 6-1-0 start, powered by Connor Hellebuyck's .952 save percentage and two shutouts, drives trader consensus favoring them at home against the Colorado Avalanche, whose odds sit at around 40% implied probability for victory. Colorado struggles without captain Nathan MacKinnon (upper-body injury, day-to-day per latest report) and faces back-to-back fatigue after a 4-3 overtime loss to Minnesota, while Jets boast top-ranked defense allowing just 1.86 goals per game. Head-to-head history favors Winnipeg (4-1-1 last six), but Avs' power play (28.6% efficiency) could exploit any Jets penalties amid rising Central Division rivalry stakes.

eventMetadata.context_description: St. Louis Blues dominate trader sentiment against the San Jose Sharks, fueled by St. Louis' four-game win streak and elite defensive play allowing just 1.75 goals per game recently, versus San Jose's league-worst 4.5 goals against average amid their rebuild. Official reports confirm Sharks star Tomas Hertl (knee) and Mario Ferraro (upper body) are out, gutting their top-six forward and blue line, while Blues forward Robert Thomas returns from injury to bolster scoring. Enterprise Center home-ice and Blues' 6-2-2 home record sharpen the edge; Sharks face back-to-back fatigue soon after, amplifying upset risk in this historically lopsided rivalry.

eventMetadata.context_description: Washington Capitals enter their home matchup against the Utah Hockey Club as heavy favorites in trader consensus, driven by Alex Ovechkin's scorching start with goals in consecutive games as he chases Wayne Gretzky's record, bolstering an offense averaging over four goals per game early in the season. Utah, rebranded from the Coyotes, shows defensive vulnerabilities, allowing 3.5 goals per contest amid integration of new talent like Danny Taylor, with goaltender Karel Vejmelka posting a .890 save percentage. No major injuries reported for either side per official updates, but Capitals' rest advantage after a day off contrasts Utah's travel fatigue from a cross-country road trip. Historical context favors Washington in home openers, though Utah's desperation for early wins could spark an upset.

events.title: Türkiye vs. Romania
events.description: This event is for the upcoming Europe World Cup Qualifiers game, scheduled for Thursday, March 26, 2026 between Türkiye and Romania.

eventMetadata.context_description: Turkey's recent dominance in UEFA Nations League play, including a convincing 3-0 away win over Iceland on October 10, bolsters trader consensus at 62.5% for a home victory against Romania in Kayseri. Hosting at Kadir Has Stadium provides a clear edge, amplified by Arda Güler's emerging form and a fully fit squad per official reports, contrasting Romania's solid but less explosive run—unbeaten in five but facing travel fatigue after their 3-0 win over Lithuania. Head-to-head history favors Turkey (3 wins in last 5), while Romania's defensive resilience keeps draw odds viable at 20.5%, though market pricing reflects wisdom-of-crowds skepticism on an upset given the hosts' momentum and superior attack.

question: Will Türkiye vs. Romania end in a draw?
description:
In the upcoming game, scheduled for March 26, 2026
If the game ends in a draw, this market will resolve to "Yes".
Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".
If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.
If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve to "Yes".
This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: Ukraine vs. Sweden
events.description: This event is for the upcoming Europe World Cup Qualifiers game, scheduled for Thursday, March 26, 2026 between Ukraine and Sweden.

eventMetadata.context_description: Sweden's home-field edge at Friends Arena anchors their 36% implied probability as trader favorite, bolstered by a deeper attacking roster featuring Alexander Isak and Dejan Kulusevski in form after recent club exploits. Yet Ukraine's gritty resilience—unbeaten in four straight Nations League qualifiers despite wartime disruptions—fuels the tight race, with their counterattacking bite via Mykhailo Mudryk and Viktor Tsygankov elevating draw odds to 31%. No major injuries reported on official lists, but Sweden's rest disadvantage after midweek travel versus Ukraine's fresher legs adds uncertainty; historical head-to-heads show low-scoring stalemates, underscoring why traders see value across all outcomes in this Group B clash.
